mechanism_narrative: Consistent expansion in nonfarm payrolls signals sustained economic growth, underpinning corporate earnings and supporting equity prices over a quarterly horizon, making rising payrolls a durable bullish signal.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative more than half the time when PAYEMS increases over a 3-month period, or Rank IC on PAYEMS momentum is below 0.03 over a rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate crossing 6.5% signals labor market weakness that erodes consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative equity returns within a week as markets reprice recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact: historically, UNRATE spikes above 6.5% preceded market declines in 2008-2009
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 6.5%, the 5-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average negative return is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI is rising and the federal funds rate remains accommodative, real interest rates fall, reducing the discount rate on equities and boosting valuations. The signal requires the joint condition of elevated inflation and low policy rates.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY > 2% and FEDFUNDS < 2%, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the condition generates positive returns less than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts are a positive leading indicator for equity markets during the early expansion phase of the business cycle, as they reflect improving construction activity and consumer confidence. In later phases, this signal may reverse or lose significance.
failure_condition: In periods identified as early expansion (e.g., initial 2 years after a trough), the correlation between HOUST changes and 60-day forward returns is not positive and statistically significant (Spearman rank correlation p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals an impending contraction in manufacturing activity, which reduces corporate profits and leads to negative equity returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact: INDPRO declines in 2001 and 2008 were followed by bear markets
failure_condition: When INDPRO YoY falls below -1%, the 20-day forward return is positive in more than 50% of cases, or the average return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in payroll employment and decreases in the unemployment rate provide the strongest signal of a healthy labor market, supporting consumer spending and equity valuations. The joint movement is more informative than either series alone.
failure_condition: When both conditions hold concurrently, the 20-day forward return is positive less than 60% of the time, or the improvement over either single-series signal is not statistically significant (paired test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail and food services sales reflect robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and signals economic momentum, leading to positive equity returns over a short weekly horizon.
failure_condition: The relationship between RSAFS momentum (3-month change) and 5-day forward returns produces a Spearman rank IC < 0.02 over a rolling 2-year window, or the average return in top-quintile months is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, rising federal funds rates compress equity valuations by increasing discount rates and borrowing costs, producing negative forward returns over a one-month horizon. The effect is most pronounced when the tightening is sustained.
failure_condition: In periods where FEDFUNDS is increasing over a 6-month window, the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the negative return rate is below 55%.
